bugs.error_message: tsc exited immediately with TS5101 (baseUrl deprecated), so `tsc --noEmit` has never actually type-checked this project's files — it always failed before reaching them.
bugs.root_cause: tsconfig.json (unchanged since initial commit) sets "baseUrl": "." without "ignoreDeprecations": "6.0", and the pinned typescript@6.0.3 treats baseUrl as a hard deprecation error, aborting before checking any file.
bugs.fix: Added "ignoreDeprecations": "6.0" to compilerOptions so tsc actually runs. This uncovered ~12 pre-existing files with real type errors that predate this session: missing theme colors (theme.cream, theme.greenPale referenced but not defined in lib/theme.ts), Badge/StatBox components used with props (children, pct) their Props types don't declare, `number && {...}` conditional style spreads (e.g. `0 && {...}`) that don't satisfy RN's style types, a StyleSheet.absoluteFillObject typo (should be absoluteFill), and a StatusBar backgroundColor prop that doesn't exist on its type. None of these are in files touched this session (app/(portal)/admin/special_tracks.tsx, app/(portal)/parent/{attendance,dashboard,homework_view,messages,recordings,timeline}.tsx, app/(portal)/student/{points,store}.tsx, app/(portal)/teacher/{evaluate,grouphomework,recordlesson}.tsx) except app/_layout.tsx and app/index.tsx which inherited the absoluteFillObject/StatusBar/global.css issues verbatim from the pre-existing code they were built from.

bugs.error_message: Hydration failed because the server rendered HTML didn't match the client. AuthGate rendered PortalScreen on server but spinner on client.
bugs.root_cause: useState initializers ran differently on server vs client: getToken() returns null on SSR (no localStorage) so isLoading=false on server, but returns a real token on client so isLoading=true. Mismatched initial render caused React hydration error.
bugs.fix: Changed isLoading initial state from !!getToken() to true (always). Changed user initial state from getStoredUser() to null. Both now agree with SSR. useEffect restores user from storage and sets isLoading=false after hydration.

bugs.error_message: Editing a teacher to add email/password does not create a User account when teacher was originally created without credentials
bugs.root_cause: updateTeacher only called userDoc.save() on existing User. If userDoc was null (teacher created without credentials) the else branch was missing — silently did nothing.
bugs.fix: Added else-if branch: when userDoc is null but email + (newPassword || password) are provided, create a new User document for the teacher.

bugs.error_message: Editing a student never creates/updates the User login account — email/password silently discarded
bugs.root_cause: updateStudent passed the entire parsed body (including email and password) to Student.findByIdAndUpdate, which Mongoose silently ignores on unknown fields. The User model was never touched. AdminStudents.tsx edit form also had no email/password fields at all.
bugs.fix: (1) Destructure email/password out before Student.findByIdAndUpdate. (2) Create or update User document when credentials provided (mirrors updateTeacher logic). (3) Enrich getStudents list with email from User collection. (4) Added email/password inputs to AdminStudents.tsx edit modal. (5) Added email field to Student TypeScript type.

bugs.error_message: User report: clicking a student's 'manage students' action on /#special_tracks (admin) sometimes doesn't open, is slow, or the click lands in the wrong place
bugs.root_cause: TrackCard, InfoRow, and SectionHeader were defined as nested function components inside AdminSpecialTracks(). Any state change on the page (typing in student search, selecting a student, opening/closing any modal) creates new component function identities each render, forcing React to fully unmount+remount every track card in the grid instead of patching props. This caused the click that opens the students modal to sometimes race the remount, producing missed clicks / perceived lag / clicks landing on the wrong element after layout re-settles.
bugs.fix: Hoisted TrackCard, InfoRow, and SectionHeader to module scope (stable component identity across renders). TrackCard now receives onManageStudents/onEdit/onDelete as props instead of closing over component state setters. Added a stable openStudents(item) helper in AdminSpecialTracks to replace the inline modal-opening closure.

bugs.error_message: git stash used to diff pre/post-change tsc errors trapped concurrent agents' work-in-progress (quran-hifz-server Phase 1 backend + quran-hifz-mobile Phase 3) in the stash; git stash pop then aborted on .wolf/anatomy.md and .wolf/memory.md conflicts, leaving both my quran-hifz edits and the other agents' backend/mobile edits present only in the stash with nothing on disk
bugs.root_cause: Ran `git stash -u` in a multi-agent repo to capture a clean tsc baseline for comparison; the stash captured the entire dirty working tree including other agents' in-flight work, and the pop step failed due to unrelated OpenWolf log-file conflicts (.wolf/anatomy.md, .wolf/memory.md are append-only and change on every agent turn)
bugs.fix: Recovered every trapped file individually with `git checkout stash@{0} -- <path>` (does not require a clean merge, unlike stash pop/apply), verified quran-hifz-server tsc still passed after recovery, then ran `git stash drop` once nothing else was needed from it

bugs.error_message: User report: teacher has zero حلقات on #myhalqa but the 'طلاب الحلقة' tab on #students shows students anyway
bugs.root_cause: TeacherStudents.tsx computed `firstHalqaId = halqat[0]?._id` and called `useStudents({ halqa: firstHalqaId })`. When a teacher has zero halqat (confirmed via direct DB query — teacher id 6a4516cfda22032acf633b87 has no Halqa document referencing it at all), firstHalqaId is undefined, and api/students.ts's buildQuery() skips any falsy filter value, so it silently sent GET /students with NO filter — returning ALL 9 students in the database (belonging to other teachers' halqat) instead of none. This made a teacher with no halqa appear to have students. Same root pattern as the earlier TeacherAttendance.tsx `firstHalqa = halqat[0]` bug fixed via ContextPicker, but this file was outside that refactor's scope and was missed. Also would have under-fetched for a teacher with 2+ halqat (only showed the first one's roster).
bugs.fix: (1) quran-hifz-server student.controller.ts getStudents: halqa query param now accepts a comma-separated list of ids, using $in when more than one. (2) quran-hifz/src/quran/api/students.ts: useStudents() now takes an optional second `{enabled}` arg passed through to react-query, so callers can explicitly gate the query instead of it silently firing with an empty filter. (3) TeacherStudents.tsx: aggregates ALL of the teacher's halqa ids (not just the first), passes them comma-joined, and gates the query on `!loadingHalqat && halqaIds.length > 0`; renders an explicit 'لا توجد حلقة مسندة لهذا المعلم' state when the teacher truly has none.

bugs.error_message: Browser console shows repeated 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warnings immediately after teacher login, plus a pageerror 'Switched to client rendering because the server rendering errored: localStorage is not defined' on first navigation to any quran-hifz page in dev mode.
bugs.root_cause: NOT YET DIAGNOSED. Confirmed via Playwright E2E during the quran-plans feature build (2026-07-03) that this occurs on EVERY page load right after login — reproduced with zero navigation into the new TeacherPlans.tsx page, so it predates and is unrelated to that feature. Likely chain: TanStack Start SSR renders ThemeProvider, which reads `localStorage` during SSR (no `typeof window` guard) → SSR throws → dev server 'switches to client rendering' → the resulting remount/rehydrate appears to trigger a setState-in-effect loop somewhere in the auth/portal/theme context chain, logged by React as 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. App still functions (login, navigation, CRUD all work correctly per E2E testing), so this looks like a dev-mode-only cosmetic console spam issue, not a functional bug — but not confirmed for production builds.
bugs.fix: NOT FIXED — out of scope for the quran-plans feature. Flagging for a future session: check ThemeContext.tsx for an SSR-unsafe `localStorage` read (needs `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard) as the likely root cause of the initial SSR error; the update-depth loop may resolve once that's fixed, or may be a separate issue in AuthContext/PortalContext worth tracing independently.

bugs.error_message: بيانات غير صالحة (Invalid data) shown every time the teacher clicked "حفظ وإرسال إشعارات" (save + notify) on the attendance page
bugs.root_cause: bulkSchema/recordSchema required client-supplied day and time strings, but neither quran-hifz nor quran-hifz-mobile attendance mutation hooks ever sent them, so Zod validation always failed with a ZodError -> generic "بيانات غير صالحة" message. Separately, the notifications half of the feature was never implemented: bulkAttendance only wrote Attendance records, never created a Message, and Message.model.ts was missing the student field that parent.controller.ts.getChildMessages already queried by (so the parent per-child Messages tab was always empty even for any manually-created message).
bugs.fix: Derive day/time server-side from date instead of requiring them in the request body. Added student field to Message schema. Added notifyAbsentParents() in attendance.controller.ts: looks up each absent student parent via ParentStudent, creates a Message per linked parent; students with no linked parent are skipped (not thrown) and returned as unnotified:[{id,name}] in the bulk response. TeacherAttendance.tsx (web) and teacher/attendance.tsx (mobile) now show a warning alert listing students whose parent could not be notified.

bugs.error_message: Re-opening the attendance page for the same halqa/track later the same day always showed every student defaulting back to "حاضر", even for students already marked "غائب" and saved earlier that day
bugs.root_cause: TeacherAttendance (web) and teacher/attendance.tsx (mobile) kept marked statuses only in local component state (statuses/setStatuses), initialized empty on every mount, and never fetched existing Attendance records for the selected context/today to seed it. So the UI looked like the previous save had been wiped, though the DB data itself was untouched.
bugs.fix: Added a useAttendance({halqa|specialTrack, from: today, to: today}) query in both files to fetch today already-saved records for the selected context, merged via a statusFor(id) helper (local override > saved-today status > "حاضر" default) so unedited students keep their previously saved status on resubmit and re-opening the page mid-day reflects reality. Renamed local state from statuses to overrides to make the merge explicit. Also added the missing enabled guard to quran-hifz-mobile lib/queries/attendance.ts useAttendance (mirroring the webs version) since this was the first call site where the filters argument could be undefined.

Linked-children chips on AdminParents page (table row, mobile card, and Link Child modal) rendered with no visible background fill
bugs.root_cause: Inline style used background: var(--bg2), but --bg2 is never defined anywhere in quran.css (only --green/--gold/--brown/--cream/--white/--surface/--text*/--border* exist) — the property silently fell back to no fill, so the chip looked like plain bordered text instead of a filled pill. Same undefined var also used once in AdminStudents.tsx (not fixed there, out of scope for this pass).
bugs.fix: Replaced all 3 occurrences with a new shared .child-chip / .child-chip-remove CSS class (using the existing var(--green-pale) fill) in quran.css, plus a dark-mode override, and extracted a module-scope ChildChip component to stop the markup being duplicated 3x in the file.

bugs.error_message: Reports page mixed Arabic-Indic digits (new widgets: ٨٣٪) with Western digits (StudentReportPanel: 83%, 10/10, juz numbers) — user flagged "problem with the arabic with the design"
bugs.root_cause: StudentReportPanel (older component) rendered plain Western digits in recharts axes/LabelList/tooltips + juz & comparison tables, while the new ReportsAnalytics widgets correctly used toAr/pct — placing them on the same page caused two digit systems to collide
bugs.fix: Converted StudentReportPanel to toAr/pct everywhere (StatsRow, juz table, comparison table) and added tickFormatter/LabelList formatter/Tooltip formatter to radar/line/bar charts so all chart numbers render Arabic-Indic; verified via Playwright diag scan (leaf text with [0-9] → numbers: [])

bugs.error_message: GET /api/quran-plans?student=__none__ and GET /api/evaluations?student=__none__ both return 500 on initial mount
bugs.root_cause: StudentReportPanel picks selectedId = pickedId || students[0]?._id || "" reactively; on first render before the students query resolves, selectedId is empty so it queries useQuranPlans/useEvaluations with the literal filter value student: "__none__" as a sentinel to mean "fetch nothing yet" — but the backend tries to treat "__none__" as a real ObjectId and throws, instead of the frontend using enabled:false to skip the request entirely.
bugs.fix: NOT FIXED — discovered during visual verification of the new ReportsDashboard.tsx (which reuses StudentReportPanel unchanged) via Playwright network capture. Harmless/self-healing (next render resolves the real selectedId and the correct data loads a moment later), so left out of scope for this session's task. A real fix should either pass enabled: false to the two hooks when selectedId is empty, or have the backend controllers 400 instead of 500 on a malformed ObjectId.

bugs.error_message: Teacher attendance day-slider showed the exact same day (weekday + day number) repeated for every chip — user: "all the day show the same days"
bugs.root_cause: addDays() built the next date via new Date(iso+"T00:00:00") (local midnight) then setDate()+toISOString() (UTC). For any timezone ahead of UTC (this dev machine: Africa/Cairo, UTC+3), local midnight of day X+1 converts to UTC as day X at 21:00 — so toISOString().split("T")[0] returned X again, a fixed point. Every loop iteration in buildDayChips() advanced cur to itself, so all 1095 chips (the guard cap) rendered the same iso/weekday/day-number. Confirmed by isolated node repro. today (new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0]) had the same UTC-vs-local-date bug on a narrower scale (wrong during the first ~3 hours of each local day).
bugs.fix: Rewrote addDays to pure UTC arithmetic: new Date(Date.UTC(y, m-1, d+n)).toISOString().split("T")[0] — never touches local time, so the offset can't leak in. Rewrote today to build from now.getFullYear()/getMonth()/getDate() (local calendar date) instead of toISOString().

bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Hero trend-delta chip on the new bento ReportsDashboard.tsx showed a nonsensical "+٥.٧" swing next to a "٧.٢/١٠" average score
bugs.root_cause: halfSplit (the half-over-half average comparison powering the hero delta chip and the 4 metric-tile arrows) only required evaluations.length >= 4 before computing a 3-vs-3 (or fewer) split. With exactly 6 real evaluation records in the test dataset, a 3-vs-3 split is noisy enough that one low outlier in one half can produce a delta larger than the average itself, which reads as broken/alarming even though the arithmetic was technically correct.
bugs.fix: Raised the minimum sample requirement from evaluations.length < 4 to < 8, guaranteeing at least 4 samples per half before any delta chip renders. Caught during Playwright verification by extracting .hero-delta/.hero-number textContent via page.evaluate() and sanity-checking the numbers against each other, not just eyeballing the screenshot.
Rewrote 17→10 lines (7 removed) | Also: {/* Dev accounts */}; <div className="login-dev">
